company.name: Legend Biotech
company.subtitle: CARVYKTI $1.9B sales · Profitability 2026 · Frontline MM expansion · In vivo CAR-T

assets.name: CARVYKTI — 2L+ MM (approved, commercial)
assets.stage: Commercial (approved US/EU/Japan/China, OS benefit in label)

market.notes: Legend 2025 revenue ~$1B ($1.9B CARVYKTI net trade sales by J&J). Q4: $306M (+64% YoY). 10K+ patients treated. 97% mfg success. <30 day TAT. 10K annualized capacity. 279 global sites.

us.patientsK.note: SEER: ~35K new MM/yr US; ~160K prevalent on active tx (long OS in era of quadruplets). Source: NCI SEER, IMF.

eu.patientsK.note: EU5 ~40K MM/yr; ~180K prevalent on-tx; EMN registry.

row.patientsK.note: ROW ~300K on-tx; China + Japan major APAC volumes.

us.wtpPct.note: ~65% -- DaraRVd quadruplet 1L NCCN Cat 1; CAR-T (Carvykti, Abecma) 2L post-CARTITUDE-4; bispecifics 4L+.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~48% -- NICE + G-BA approved DaraRVd + CAR-T + bispecifics; IMWG guidelines.

row.wtpPct.note: ~16% -- Japan PMDA full; China NRDL daratumumab + lenalidomide generic; EM Vel/Dex/Len.

us.priceK.note: Blended $235K: DaraRVd $220K (45% pts) + CAR-T $470K (10%) + bispecific (Tecvayli/Elrexfio/Talvey) $280K (15%) + Vd/Rd $95K (30%).

penPct.note: ~58% peak -- quadruplet 1L + CAR-T 2L + bispecifics 4L fill ladder; GPRC5D + FcRH5 extend; long OS grows prevalence.

us.reachPct.note: US 2L+ R/R myeloma: ~22% of 35K = ~7.7K pts/yr reached. Carvykti already treating ~5K+/yr and growing with manufacturing scale-up + 4L->2L label expansion.

us.wtpPct.note: Strong and growing: OS benefit in label, CARTITUDE-4 data, expanding from academic to community oncology.

eu.reachPct.note: EU 2L+ access expanding; certified centers growing but still fewer than US.

eu.wtpPct.note: EMA approved; HTA negotiations ongoing in key markets.

eu.priceK.note: EU net price ~50K (discount vs US list).

market.notes: Frontline MM drug market ~$15-20B total. CAR-T addressable subset ~$8B (transplant-eligible + ineligible candidates). CARTITUDE-5 + 6 both enrolled. CARTITUDE-4 showed 50.4mo median PFS in 2L+ — strong rationale for earlier use. Legend receives mfg revenue + profit share from J&J.

us.patientsK.note: ~20K US newly dx MM/yr starting systemic tx (transplant-eligible + ineligible). Source: NCI SEER 2024, ASH.

eu.patientsK.note: EU5 ~24K new MM/yr; ~75% receive triplet/quadruplet 1L per EMN.

row.patientsK.note: ROW ~80K starting 1L; VRd + daratumumab global standard.

us.wtpPct.note: ~72% -- DaraRVd PERSEUS + IsaKRd IMROZ TE + DaraVMP non-TE; NCCN Cat 1; Medicare Part B+D.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~55% -- NICE TA approved DaraVMP + DaraRVd; France ASMR III; HTA values MRD-negativity.

row.wtpPct.note: ~18% -- Japan PMDA full dara-based; China NRDL dara 2021; EM VRd.

us.priceK.note: Blended $225K: DaraRVd $220K (60% pts) + IsaKRd $240K (15%) + DaraVMP non-TE $195K (15%) + VRd alone $145K (10%).

eu.priceK.note: ~$135K blended; EU net ~60% US; len generic reduces triplet cost.

row.priceK.note: ~$55K blended; len generic + biosim dara lower APAC/EM.

penPct.note: ~68% peak -- quadruplet 1L saturated TE; non-TE DaraVMP/VRd-lite standard; MRD-guided de-escalation emerging.

us.reachPct.note: CARTITUDE-5/6 frontline eligible; ~12% of 20K new pts/yr = 2.4K, growing with mfg scale-up

us.wtpPct.note: Proven OS benefit in 2L+; frontline adoption depends on CARTITUDE-5 data vs standard transplant

eu.reachPct.note: Frontline EU access later than US; CARTITUDE-6 supports filing

row.reachPct.note: Japan frontline feasible; China competitive landscape different

market.notes: Dual CD19/CD20. Allogeneic = off-the-shelf. If validated, transforms CAR-T economics.

us.patientsK.note: SEER: ~30K new DLBCL/yr US (most aggressive NHL subtype); ~40K prevalent on-tx. Source: NCI SEER, LLS.

row.patientsK.note: ROW ~120K on-tx; China NHL registry + Japan NCR.

us.wtpPct.note: ~68% -- Polivy+R-CHP 1L (POLARIX) NCCN Cat 1; CAR-T 2L (Yescarta/Breyanzi ZUMA-7/TRANSFORM); bispecifics 3L.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~50% -- NICE approved Polivy 1L + CAR-T 2L; G-BA betraechtlich; rituximab biosim baseline.

row.wtpPct.note: ~15% -- Japan PMDA full; China NRDL R-CHOP + domestic CAR-T; EM R-CHOP biosim.

us.priceK.note: Blended $195K: Polivy+R-CHP $175K (45% pts) + CAR-T 2L $460K (15%) + bispecific (Epkinly/Columvi) $250K (15%) + R-CHOP $40K (25%).

penPct.note: ~52% peak -- Polivy 1L penetrated 50%+; CAR-T 2L growing; bispecifics 3L fixed-duration; ADCs (ZW191) emerge.

us.reachPct.note: Allogeneic = off-the-shelf but Phase 1; only R/R DLBCL initially

us.priceK.note: Allo CAR-T pricing target ~$350K (lower than autologous ~$410K)

eu.reachPct.note: Phase 1 -- no EU access expected before 2030

market.notes: Dual CD20/CD19 in vivo — injection, no manufacturing. First patient dosed. Data H2 2026. Multiple INDs planned. Paradigm shift if it works.

us.patientsK.note: ~20K US potential if in-vivo CAR-T broadens to non-eligible (unfit, rural); frontier modality. Source: Capstan/Umoja pipeline.

eu.patientsK.note: EU5 ~22K potential; decentralization + no-apheresis model bypasses center bottleneck.

row.patientsK.note: ROW ~50K if in-vivo reaches EM; mRNA/LNP-CAR or AAV-CAR lowers CoGS.

us.wtpPct.note: ~50% -- pre-commercial; model assumes Medicare + commercial if outpatient-compatible lower-toxicity profile.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~40% -- HTA likely to value access expansion; EMA ATMP pathway.

row.wtpPct.note: ~15% -- decentralization opens APAC/EM markets currently excluded from ex-vivo.

us.priceK.note: Blended $220K: in-vivo CAR-T est $220K target (vs $500K ex-vivo); lower CoGS + infusion-only delivery.

eu.priceK.note: ~$140K blended; EU HTA likely more favorable to cost-effective in-vivo.

row.priceK.note: ~$55K blended; EM-accessible pricing key to broad launch.

penPct.note: ~38% peak -- transformational if clinical delivers; 2028+ timeline assumes Capstan/Umoja POC; 5yr ramp post launch.

us.reachPct.note: Preclinical in vivo CAR-T; earliest market ~2032; initial niche R/R

us.priceK.note: In vivo could be much cheaper than ex vivo (~$200K target)

mega_bear.narrative: CAR-T class safety signal / franchise collapse
events: ✗ FDA black box warning on secondary malignancies across CAR-T class
✗ CMS restricts reimbursement for CAR-T
✗ CARVYKTI revenue collapses, J&J deprioritizes
→ Stock re-rates to cash floor, pipeline worthless

bear.narrative: CARVYKTI growth stalls + frontline fails + competition erodes
events: ✗ CARVYKTI growth decelerates — bispecifics take share
✗ CARTITUDE-5/6 underwhelm vs standard of care
✗ Arcellx anito-cel approved, takes 2L+ share
→ Revenue plateaus at ~$1.2B Legend share

base.narrative: CARVYKTI grows + frontline expands TAM + platform validates
events: ✓ CARVYKTI revenue grows to ~$3B+ total (Legend ~$1.5B)
✓ CARTITUDE-5 or -6 positive — frontline label expansion
~ Allogeneic shows promise, needs more data
~ In vivo early data generates excitement

bull.narrative: CARVYKTI blockbuster + frontline SOC + in vivo paradigm shift
events: ✓ CARVYKTI total sales >$5B (Legend >$2B)
✓ Both CARTITUDE-5 + 6 positive — frontline standard
✓ In vivo CAR-T data transformative
✓ Autoimmune CAR-T expansion (SLE, MS)

psychedelic_bull.narrative: In vivo + autoimmune = platform acquisition at massive premium
events: ✓ CARVYKTI >$7B total sales, dominant in MM
✓ In vivo CAR-T works across oncology + autoimmune
✓ Autoimmune TAM unlock (SLE, MS, RA) = $50B+ opportunity
✓ J&J acquires Legend at 3-5x current price

lastData.title: CARVYKTI Q4 $555M sales + full-year profitability
points: Q4 CARVYKTI net trade sales: $555M (+66% YoY, +6% QoQ)
FY2025 CARVYKTI sales: $1.9B total (Legend total revenue ~$1B)
Q4 Legend revenue: $306M (+64% YoY), gross margin 61%
10,000+ patients treated, 97% mfg success, <30 day TAT
CARTITUDE-5 and CARTITUDE-6 both fully enrolled
In vivo CAR-T: first patient dosed, data expected H2 2026
LUCAR-G39D allogeneic: encouraging Phase 1 at ASH 2025
Stock at $17 vs $45 twelve months ago

nextCatalyst.title: In vivo CAR-T first-in-human data at major medical meeting
thresholds: Safety + initial efficacy of in vivo CD20/CD19 CAR-T
If positive: paradigm shift (injection vs 30-day manufacturing)
Also: multiple IND filings for oncology + autoimmune

risks: J&J collaboration — Legend gets ~50% of value, not 100%
Arcellx anito-cel gaining traction as BCMA CAR-T competitor
Bispecific antibodies eroding CAR-T share in later lines
Manufacturing complexity — capacity may not suffice for frontline
Stock down 60% despite strong fundamentals — what does market know?
In vivo CAR-T very early — high scientific risk

catalysts.title: In vivo CAR-T platform first-in-human readouts
catalysts._rationale: First-in-human in vivo dosing completed; in vivo readouts expected 2H 2026 per J.P. Morgan presentation
